description:

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm run build` completes with no warnings or errors

2. Build

## Notes

title: Wire reset.css and typography.css into src/index.js
description:
## Context
The new reset + typography files must be imported globally for the tokens system to take visual effect. Foundation layer keeps MUI CssBaseline untouched (additive only).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/index.js imports "./design/reset.css"
- [ ] src/index.js imports "./design/typography.css"
- [ ] Both imports appear near the top of the file, before App import
- [ ] MUI CssBaseline remains in place (not removed)
- [ ] App still renders; no console errors; build succeeds

## Implementation Steps
1. Open src/index.js
2. Add `import "./design/reset.css";` and `import "./design/typography.css";` above the App import (after React/ReactDOM imports)
3. Do NOT remove any existing CssBaseline, typeface-roboto, or theme-provider code
4. Run `npm start` locally and confirm page still loads; run `npm run build` and confirm success

## Files
- src/index.js

## Notes
Depends on tokens/reset/typography files already landing. Ordering matters only in that our reset/typography come after MUI baseline so they take precedence where they overlap.

title: Inventory @mui/icons-material usages in src/components/icons/INVENTORY.md
description:
## Context
Before we can replace MUI icons with custom SVGs we need an exhaustive list of every icon currently imported. Each icon will later get its own child task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/components/icons/ directory exists
- [ ] src/components/icons/INVENTORY.md exists
- [ ] INVENTORY.md lists every unique @mui/icons-material icon used anywhere in src/
- [ ] For each icon: name, number of import sites, one example file path
- [ ] No icon SVGs are implemented yet (that is child-task work)

## Implementation Steps
1. Create directory src/components/icons/
2. Run: `grep -rhE "from \"@mui/icons-material" src/ | sort -u` and related `grep -rnE "@mui/icons-material/[A-Za-z]+" src/`
3. Parse the output to extract unique icon names
4. For each icon record count + one example path
5. Write results to src/components/icons/INVENTORY.md as a markdown table
6. Add a header section listing total unique icons and total import sites

## Files
- src/components/icons/INVENTORY.md

## Notes
Inventory-only. Do NOT create .jsx icon components yet.

title: Create src/design/typography.css with fontsource imports + utility classes
description:
## Context
Fontsource requires explicit imports per weight. We also want small utility classes (.t-h1 .. .t-mono) so non-primitive code can opt in to the new type system without inline styles.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/design/typography.css imports @fontsource/space-grotesk/400.css, 600.css, 800.css
- [ ] Imports @fontsource/jetbrains-mono/400.css and 600.css
- [ ] Defines utility classes: .t-h1, .t-h2, .t-h3, .t-h4, .t-body, .t-caption, .t-mono, .t-label
- [ ] Sizes match TYPOGRAPHY.sizes in tokens.js (h1=32, h2=24, h3=20, h4=16, body=14, caption=12)
- [ ] .t-mono uses JetBrains Mono stack; everything else uses Space Grotesk stack
- [ ] .t-label uppercase, letter-spacing: 0.05em, font-weight: 600, size 12

## Implementation Steps
1. Create src/design/typography.css
2. Add @import statements for each fontsource weight file
3. Add the utility class selectors with matching font-size / font-weight / line-height
4. Keep line-heights tight (1.1 for h1/h2, 1.2 for h3/h4, 1.4 body)

## Files
- src/design/typography.css

## Notes
Will be imported from src/index.js in a later task.

title: Create src/design/reset.css minimal CSS reset
description:
## Context
We need a minimal CSS reset that sets our baseline (black on white, border-box, Space Grotesk body) without nuking everything MUI CssBaseline does. Foundation layer only adds, does not subtract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/design/reset.css exists
- [ ] `*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; }` applied
- [ ] body and all headings have margin: 0; padding: 0
- [ ] body: font-family uses Space Grotesk stack with system-ui fallback, color:#000, background:#fff
- [ ] kbd, code, pre, samp: font-family uses JetBrains Mono stack

## Implementation Steps
1. Create src/design/reset.css
2. Write selectors above with concrete values
3. Do not add normalization that conflicts with MUI CssBaseline (leave list-style, buttons, etc. alone for now)

## Files
- src/design/reset.css

## Notes
Pure CSS file. Will be imported from src/index.js in a later task.

title: Create src/design/tokens.js with design token exports
description:
## Context
Need JS-accessible design tokens plus a CSS-variables injection string. Primitives import from tokens.js; the CSS var string is applied once globally.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/design/tokens.js exports named consts: COLORS, SPACING, TYPOGRAPHY, BORDERS, SHADOWS, MOTION
- [ ] Values match STYLE.md
- [ ] Also exports a CSS variables string (e.g. `export const cssVariables = ":root { --color-fg: #000; ... }"`) ready to inject via a <style> tag or appended to index.css

## Implementation Steps
1. Create src/design/tokens.js
2. Define:
   - COLORS = { fg:"#000", bg:"#fff", accent:"#4ed1f8", error:"#ff3030", muted:"#666" }
   - SPACING = { xs:4, sm:8, md:16, lg:24, xl:32 }
   - TYPOGRAPHY = { ui:"\"Space Grotesk\", system-ui, sans-serif", mono:"\"JetBrains Mono\", ui-monospace, monospace", sizes:{h1:32,h2:24,h3:20,h4:16,body:14,caption:12} }
   - BORDERS = { thin:"1px solid #000", thick:"2px solid #000", radius:0 }
   - SHADOWS = { hover:"2px 2px 0 0 #000" }
   - MOTION = { duration:"80ms", easing:"linear" }
3. Build cssVariables string from the above
4. Re-export everything as a default object too for convenience

## Files
- src/design/tokens.js

## Notes
Keep flat + serializable. No functions inside the objects.

title: Create src/design/ directory and STYLE.md brutalist style guide
description:
## Context
Need a single source of truth for the new brutalist/minimalist visual language. Everyone implementing primitives should be able to open STYLE.md and know exactly what 1px border color, spacing unit, hover shadow offset, etc. to use.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/design/ directory exists
- [ ] src/design/STYLE.md exists and documents every rule below
- [ ] Doc includes: border-radius = 0; borders = 1px or 2px solid #000; palette = black/white monochrome + #4ed1f8 accent + #ff3030 error; spacing scale 4/8/16/24/32; hover shadow = hard 2px-offset, no blur; no gradients anywhere; transitions = linear 80ms; focus ring = 2px solid black; type stack = Space Grotesk (UI) / JetBrains Mono (code)

## Implementation Steps
1. Create directory src/design/
2. Write src/design/STYLE.md with sections: Principles, Color, Typography, Spacing, Borders, Shadows, Motion, Focus
3. For each section list concrete values and a short "do / do not" bullet

## Files
- src/design/STYLE.md

## Notes
Doc-only; no code impact yet. This is the reference for tokens.js and every primitive.

title: Build fails: pages/index.js cannot resolve barrel import '../components/ui'
description:
`npm run build` fails after merge of gv-59b (a038b17). webpack cannot resolve '../components/ui' from src/pages/index.js because the file is being treated as strict ESM (likely due to dolt/peggy-generated dotParser.js with type=module side-effect, or an updated react-scripts config). 

Error: `Module not found: Error: Can't resolve '../components/ui' in '/Users/lou/gt/gviz/polecats/capable/gviz/src/pages'`. 'BREAKING CHANGE: The request '../components/ui' failed to resolve only because it was resolved as fully specified... Add the extension to the request.'

Fix: change import to './components/ui/index.js' (or specific file imports). Other files using bare `./components/ui` may also hit this — needs sweep. Pre-existed before gv-48t (TextEditor migration); reproduced on clean main.

title: Capture baseline bundle size + screenshot checklist
description:
## Context
Before we start ripping out MUI we need a recorded baseline so regressions (bundle size bloat, visual drift) are detectable. Screenshots of every dialog/menu/page state anchor visual QA against the new look.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `npm run build` runs to completion
- [ ] src/design/BASELINE.md exists and records sizes (KB, gzipped if available) of each file in build/static/js/*.js and build/static/css/*.css
- [ ] src/design/baseline-screenshots/ directory exists
- [ ] src/design/baseline-screenshots/CHECKLIST.md lists each page/state that needs a screenshot taken manually

## Implementation Steps
1. Run `npm run build`
2. `ls -la build/static/js` and `ls -la build/static/css` — record sizes
3. Write src/design/BASELINE.md with a table: filename | raw size | gzip size (if available)
4. Create src/design/baseline-screenshots/
5. Write CHECKLIST.md listing every distinct UI state to screenshot: main graph view, empty state, each dialog (open/save/settings/etc.), each menu (file/edit/view), error toast, loading state, mobile viewport
6. Screenshots themselves are captured manually by a human — this task only sets up the list

## Files
- src/design/BASELINE.md
- src/design/baseline-screenshots/CHECKLIST.md

## Notes
P1 because not blocking but must happen before we start deletion work.

Migrate src/ButtonAppBar.js (303 lines) from MUI to src/components/ui/* primitives. The file currently imports:
- from @mui/material: AppBar, Toolbar, Typography, Button, IconButton, Icon
- from tss-react/mui: withStyles
- from @mui/icons-material (10 icons): Menu, Add, OpenInBrowser, SaveAlt, Undo, Redo, ZoomIn, ZoomOut, ZoomOutMap, Settings, Help

Must preserve every event handler and prop wiring.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No @mui imports remain in src/ButtonAppBar.js
- [ ] No tss-react import
- [ ] All MUI components swapped to src/components/ui/*
- [ ] All 10 icons swapped to hand-rolled SVGs in src/components/icons/<Name>.jsx
- [ ] Custom styles converted to inline style + tokens or colocated CSS module
- [ ] Cypress + jest tests touching this file still pass
- [ ] Visual: matches brutalist style guide

## Implementation Steps
1. Replace `from '@mui/material'` with `from './components/ui'` (AppBar->TopBar, Toolbar->Toolbar, Typography->Text, Button, IconButton, Icon)
2. Replace `withStyles(ButtonAppBar, styles)` with default export of ButtonAppBar
3. Convert the `styles` object to inline style + tokens or colocated CSS module
4. Replace icons: Menu, Add, OpenInBrowser, SaveAlt, Undo, Redo, ZoomIn, ZoomOut, ZoomOutMap, Settings, Help (blocked on child icon beads under gv-4ddec741)
5. Smoke test: `npm test -- ButtonAppBar`
6. Manual verify in browser

title: mol-polecat-work
description:
Full polecat work lifecycle from assignment through completion.

This molecule guides a polecat through a complete work assignment. Each step
has clear entry/exit criteria and specific commands to run. A polecat can
crash after any step and resume from the last completed step.

## Polecat Contract (Self-Cleaning Model)

You are a self-cleaning worker. You:
1. Receive work via your hook (formula checklist + issue)
2. Work through formula steps in order (shown inline at prime time)
3. Complete and self-clean via `gt done` (submit + nuke yourself)
4. You are GONE - Refinery merges from MQ

**Self-cleaning:** When you run `gt done`, you push your work, submit to MQ,
nuke your sandbox, and exit. There is no idle state. Done means gone.

**Important:** This formula defines the workflow template. Steps are shown inline
when you run `gt prime` — there are no separate step beads to close. Work through
the checklist, then run `gt done`.

**Speed principle:** You run the full gate suite AFTER rebasing onto the target
branch (pre-verify step). This enables the refinery to fast-path merge your MR
in ~5 seconds instead of re-running gates. If pre-verification is skipped or
stale, the refinery falls through to normal gate execution.

**You do NOT:**
- Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
- Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
- Fix pre-existing failures on main (Refinery owns main health)

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | hook_bead | The issue ID you're assigned to work on |
| base_branch | sling vars | The base branch to rebase on (default: main) |
| setup_command | rig config | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | rig config | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | rig config | Test command. Empty = skip. Rig must configure for its language. |
| lint_command | rig config | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| build_command | rig config | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Build fails | Fix it. Do not proceed if it won't compile. |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

title: mol-polecat-work
description:
Full polecat work lifecycle from assignment through completion.

This molecule guides a polecat through a complete work assignment. Each step
has clear entry/exit criteria and specific commands to run. A polecat can
crash after any step and resume from the last completed step.

## Polecat Contract (Self-Cleaning Model)

You are a self-cleaning worker. You:
1. Receive work via your hook (formula checklist + issue)
2. Work through formula steps in order (shown inline at prime time)
3. Complete and self-clean via `gt done` (submit + nuke yourself)
4. You are GONE - Refinery merges from MQ

**Self-cleaning:** When you run `gt done`, you push your work, submit to MQ,
nuke your sandbox, and exit. There is no idle state. Done means gone.

**Important:** This formula defines the workflow template. Steps are shown inline
when you run `gt prime` — there are no separate step beads to close. Work through
the checklist, then run `gt done`.

**Speed principle:** You run the full gate suite AFTER rebasing onto the target
branch (pre-verify step). This enables the refinery to fast-path merge your MR
in ~5 seconds instead of re-running gates. If pre-verification is skipped or
stale, the refinery falls through to normal gate execution.

**You do NOT:**
- Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
- Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
- Fix pre-existing failures on main (Refinery owns main health)

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | hook_bead | The issue ID you're assigned to work on |
| base_branch | sling vars | The base branch to rebase on (default: main) |
| setup_command | rig config | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | rig config | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | rig config | Test command. Empty = skip. Rig must configure for its language. |
| lint_command | rig config | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| build_command | rig config | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Build fails | Fix it. Do not proceed if it won't compile. |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

value: Brutalist redesign: monochrome + single #4ed1f8 accent because the editor surface is colorful (graphs); chrome must not compete.
Motion: linear 80ms transitions only; no easing curves. Brutalism rejects organic movement. Hover lift is a hard 2px-offset shadow with no blur.

Typography: Space Grotesk for UI / JetBrains Mono for code-adjacent labels — the app is a code editor; mono labels reinforce the medium. Note: @fontsource/space-grotesk publishes weights 300-700 only (no 800); use 700 as the bold weight.

UI redesign master epic gviz-2kk migrates from @mui/material to Base UI + brutalist styling across 22 component files; functionality must remain byte-identical. 138+ beads filed. Key sub-epics: gviz-95g (Foundation), gviz-zv3 (Primitives), gviz-0x8 (Layout), gviz-8vf (Dialogs), gviz-q0l (Surfaces), gviz-rdr (QA), gviz-3gq (Cleanup).
